rows.N: Moderately differentiated invasive non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma (stage IV-A)
rows.O: SITE: ORAL CAVITY, SUB-SITE: MOBILE TONGUE, RIGHT SIDE, cT4acNIcMO Stage IVa

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Moderately differentiated invasive non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma, site oral cavity, sub-site mobile tongue right side, staging cT4acNIcMO, Stage Four-A (IVa)
fields.SECONDARY_DIAGNOSES: Costal wall metastasis (sixth left posterior costal arch — pericondral and trabecular bone metastatic infiltration); incisional supraumbilical hernia with entero-cutaneous (gastric) fistula managed conservatively
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Patient Sandra Elizabeth León Valdez, 70 years old, affiliate of the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social. Diagnosed with moderately differentiated invasive non-keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity (mobile tongue, right side), cT4acNIcMO Stage Four-A. Initially treated with chemoradiation: 2 cycles of cisplatin and 69 Gy (2.3 Gy, 30 fractions), completed 28 April 2024. Subsequently underwent salvage surgery on 6 December 2024: right partial glossectomy (wide resection with negative margins confirmed by intraoperative study), selective supraomohyoid dissection levels I-V right, Stamm-type gastrostomy, metastasectomy with partial resection of the sixth left posterior costal arch (oligometastasis). Postoperative pathology: squamous cell carcinoma, moderately differentiated, ulcerated, invasive (depth of invasion 1.7 cm), TNM categorization pT3yN0, with perineural invasion, surgical margins free of neoplasia; sixth left posterior costal arch metastasectomy showing pericondral metastatic infiltration and infiltration in trabecular bone spaces, with margins free of metastasis and negative lymph nodes. Clinically without local tumor activity, good tongue healing, negative neck. Later developed pain in the left posterior costal wall of 2 weeks evolution; computed tomography reported a new lesion in the costal arch superior to the prior resection, with soft tissue involvement and linear fracture — localized disease. Pulmonary window without lesions. Received palliative radiotherapy to the costal lesion. Pending initiation of systemic treatment for distant recurrence; multidisciplinary discussion with medical oncology identified greater benefit from immunotherapy.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Pain in the left posterior costal wall of 2 weeks evolution
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Stage Four-A (IVa) squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity with distant recurrence (costal metastasis) after chemoradiation and salvage surgery; disease considered locally controlled but with oligometastatic distant recurrence requiring systemic immunotherapy
fields.COMORBIDITIES: Incisional supraumbilical hernia with entero-cutaneous (gastric) fistula managed conservatively
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Chemoradiation: 2 cycles of cisplatin plus 69 Gy (2.3 Gy, 30 fractions), completed 28 April 2024; salvage surgery on 6 December 2024 including right partial glossectomy, selective supraomohyoid dissection levels I-V right, Stamm-type gastrostomy, metastasectomy with partial resection of sixth left posterior costal arch; palliative radiotherapy to costal lesion
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: Disease recurred at distance (new costal lesion superior to prior resection site) after chemoradiation and salvage surgery, requiring systemic treatment
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Sixth left posterior costal arch (oligometastasis — pericondral and trabecular bone infiltration); new costal lesion with soft tissue involvement and linear fracture detected at follow-up imaging
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Oncological surgeon Dr. Karen Girón Orellana certified that the patient, after chemoradiation and salvage surgery with current distant recurrence, has no local tumor activity but shows a new costal lesion on tomography. Multidisciplinary discussion with medical oncology identified greater benefit from immunotherapy. The treating physician recommended Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg every 3 weeks indefinitely as the most appropriate systemic treatment for the distant recurrence.
fields.URGENCY: Urgent — the prescribing physician indicated the need to begin systemic treatment urgently; the court granted provisional amparo ordering immediate supply of the medication; the Constitutional Court confirmed that exhausting administrative procedures was not required given the susceptibility and gravity of the rights at stake
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: High risk — untreated distant recurrence of squamous cell carcinoma at the costal wall with evidence of disease progression; failure to treat could allow further progression of the malignancy
fields.RISK_HOSPITALIZATION: NOT FOUND explicitly, but implied by the severity of recurrent metastatic disease
fields.RISK_DEATH: High risk — the court noted that the patient's life and health rights were at imminent risk; the disease was described as grave and aggressive, threatening severe damage if not adequately treated
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks, indefinitely, as prescribed by Dr. Karen Girón Orellana (colegiado 14,223)
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Court confirmed the first-instance amparo grant, holding that the rights to life and health are fundamental rights that the State must guarantee through all available means, including via the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social. The Court applied the dispositivo principle, recognizing that when an affiliate has adequate medical backing — here a certificate and prescription by a specialist in oncological surgery — it is appropriate to honor the patient's preference for a specific medication under the responsibility of the patient and the prescribing physician. The Court dismissed the Institute's arguments regarding lack of passive legitimation, non-exhaustion of administrative remedies, and limitations imposed by public procurement law (Ley de Contrataciones del Estado), holding that the fundamental rights to life and health prevail over formal administrative or economic arguments. The Court further noted that ordering a specific drug does not constitute judicial prescription of medicine, but rather is a constitutional determination grounded in the physician's expert recommendation and the patient's preference. As a special consideration, the Court underscored that the 70-year-old patient is entitled to reinforced state protection as an elderly person, requiring continuous, comprehensive, permanent and efficient health services.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala, artículos 1°, 2°, 3°, 93, 94, 95, 100, 265, 268, 272 inciso c); Ley de Amparo, Exhibición Personal y de Constitucionalidad, artículos 8°, 10, 42, 43, 44, 45, 46, 47, 49, 50, 52, 53, 54, 55, 60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 66, 67, 149, 156, 163 inciso c), 179, 185; Acuerdo 3-89, artículo 7 Bis; Acuerdo 1-2013 de la Corte de Constitucionalidad, artículos 36, 46; Pacto Internacional de Derechos Económicos Sociales y Culturales, artículo 12; Declaración Americana de Derechos y Deberes del Hombre, artículo XI; jurisprudencia de la Corte de Constitucionalidad: expedientes 3231-2025, 4220-2025, 4234-2025, 2918-2025, 3989-2025, 4211-2025, 4014-2025, 4796-2025, 3932-2025, 2821-2025, 2145-2025, 4407-2025, 1444-2025, 4240-2025, 5185-2025, 1952-2025, 2033-2025, 2018-2025, 2833-2025, 1990-2025, 2907-2025, 1350-2025, 2837-2025, 2392-2025, 550-2025, 2219-2025, 4234-2025, 8940-2024, 3790-2025, 4407-2025, 7069-2024, 3677-2025, 3822-2025

missing_from_no: The N/O columns do not capture: (1) the patient's age (70 years); (2) the detailed prior treatment history (chemoradiation regimen, salvage surgery with intraoperative margin study, specific lymph node dissection levels, gastrostomy, metastasectomy details, palliative radiotherapy); (3) the pathological staging (pT3yN0, perineural invasion, depth of invasion 1.7 cm); (4) the nature and location of the distant recurrence (new costal lesion with soft tissue involvement and linear fracture, distinct from the previously resected costal arch); (5) the multidisciplinary oncology discussion supporting the immunotherapy choice; (6) the comorbidity of incisional hernia with entero-cutaneous fistula; (7) the urgency characterization and imminent risk to life; (8) the judicial decision and grounds.
possibly_incorrect: Column O states 'cT4acNIcMO Stage IVa' — the document confirms this is the initial clinical staging, which is accurate. Column N accurately reflects the primary diagnosis name. However, N omits the distant recurrence status (oligometastasis/costal wall relapse) which is the clinical context triggering the amparo; strictly speaking the staging at the time of the amparo is beyond the initial IVa designation due to distant relapse. No direct factual conflict, but the metastatic description in O ('NI' was not used here) is absent from N.

rows.N: Renal Cancer Stage IV with Pulmonary Metastases

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Renal cancer Stage Four (EC IV) with pulmonary metastases — histopathology: clear cell renal carcinoma
fields.SECONDARY_DIAGNOSES: Subdiaphragmatic lesion identified on PET-CT (fluorodeoxyglucose) — not further specified
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Patient Ramón Escobar Barrera, 74 years old, affiliate of the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social. In 2017 underwent nephrectomy; histopathological report confirmed clear cell renal carcinoma. After surgery, clinical surveillance was maintained. In May 2022, thoracic computed tomography revealed pulmonary lesions suggestive of metastases; complementary PET-CT (fluorodeoxyglucose) confirmed the metastatic lesions and an additional subdiaphragmatic lesion. Local control of these lesions was achieved with radiotherapy. In September 2022 the patient started Pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenously every 21 days and Axitinib 5 mg orally every 12 hours; due to toxicity, the dose of Axitinib was reduced to 5 mg orally. Latest restaging computed tomography performed in November 2022 reported partial response to treatment. At the time of the medical certificate, the patient was being treated with Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg intravenously every three weeks and Axitinib (Inlyta) 5 mg (one tablet) orally daily on a continuous basis. The treating physician recommended continuing this treatment until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity.
fields.SYMPTOMS: NOT FOUND explicitly in the document
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Stage Four (EC IV) renal carcinoma with pulmonary and subdiaphragmatic metastases; previously in active treatment with combination immunotherapy and targeted therapy with documented partial response; rapid disease progression if treatment is interrupted
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Nephrectomy in 2017; radiotherapy for local control of pulmonary and subdiaphragmatic metastatic lesions (2022); Pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenously every 21 days combined with Axitinib 5 mg orally every 12 hours (from September 2022, with dose reduction of Axitinib to 5 mg daily due to toxicity)
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: The patient ceased to respond to initial treatments and developed pulmonary metastases after nephrectomy and clinical surveillance; the combination of Pembrolizumab and Axitinib was initiated due to metastatic progression and showed partial response
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Pulmonary (multiple metastatic lesions); subdiaphragmatic lesion (identified on PET-CT)
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Oncologist Dr. Mónica Lily Cordón Paz (colegiado 16,737) certified that the patient has Stage Four renal cancer with pulmonary metastases and was already in active treatment with Pembrolizumab and Axitinib, with the latest restaging showing partial response. She recommended continuing the combination treatment until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity. The Institute's refusal to supply these medications — which were already in use and showing benefit — jeopardized the patient's health and life.
fields.URGENCY: Urgent — the patient stated the disease advances very rapidly; the court noted that delaying administrative procedures would put the patient's health and life at risk; provisional amparo was granted immediately
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: High risk — rapid disease progression with pulmonary metastases; the patient had already progressed after nephrectomy and radiotherapy, and the combination therapy was the active treatment showing partial response
fields.RISK_DEATH: High risk — the court and the patient stated that the disease threatens the patient's life; the refusal to supply the medications was described as a threat to the most fundamental human right: life
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg intravenously every three weeks combined with Axitinib (Inlyta) 5 mg (one tablet) orally daily, continuously, as prescribed by Dr. Mónica Lily Cordón Paz (colegiado 16,737)
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Court upheld the first-instance amparo, holding that the State has the obligation to guarantee the fundamental rights to life and health through all available means, including through the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social. Applying the dispositivo principle, the Court found that when an affiliate presents adequate medical backing — specifically a certificate and prescription by a specialist oncologist documenting active treatment with partial response — it is procedurally appropriate to honor the patient's preference for the specific medications under the responsibility of the patient and the treating physician. The Court dismissed the Institute's arguments regarding administrative autonomy, the prohibition on purchasing branded medications under the Ley de Contrataciones del Estado, and the claim that a certificate and prescription are insufficient evidence, holding that fundamental rights to life and health prevail over administrative and economic considerations. The Court also rejected the argument that the amparo should be denied because no formal administrative request had been filed, reiterating that given the gravity of the rights at stake, affiliates need not exhaust administrative remedies before seeking constitutional protection.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala, artículos 3°, 93, 94, 95, 100, 265, 268, 272 literal c); Ley de Amparo, Exhibición Personal y de Constitucionalidad, artículos 8°, 45, 49, 50, 51, 52, 53, 54, 55, 60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 66, 67, 149, 150, 163 literal c), 185; Acuerdo 1-2013 de la Corte de Constitucionalidad, artículos 36, 44, 46; jurisprudencia de la Corte de Constitucionalidad: expedientes 7385-2023, 1472-2023, 4696-2023, 5781-2023, 4657-2023, 6246-2023, 6798-2023, 1901-2022, 1094-2022, 1900-2022, 4368-2022, 3407-2022, 1569-2021, 3428-2022, 2295-2021, 6230-2022, 1474-2023

missing_from_no: Column O is marked 'NI' (no information). The document contains extensive clinical information absent from both columns: (1) patient age (74 years); (2) histopathological diagnosis (clear cell renal carcinoma); (3) complete treatment history including nephrectomy in 2017, radiotherapy for metastatic lesions, and active treatment with Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib since September 2022 with dose reduction due to toxicity; (4) partial radiological response on restaging computed tomography of November 2022; (5) subdiaphragmatic lesion identified on PET-CT; (6) the specific dosing regimen (Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 21 days; Axitinib 5 mg orally daily); (7) urgency and risk to life; (8) judicial decision and grounds.
possibly_incorrect: No conflicts detected between N/O and the document. Column N ('Renal Cancer Stage IV with Pulmonary Metastases') accurately summarizes the primary diagnosis and main metastatic site as documented.

rows.O: Post-right nephrectomy (kidney removal) for a 5.5 cm mass with areas of necrosis.

fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Patient Herman Horacio Mendía Alarcón, affiliate of the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social. Diagnosed with papillary transitional cell carcinoma (urothelial). Underwent right nephrectomy in the private sector for a renal mass of 5.5 cm; pathology confirmed papillary transitional cell (urothelial) carcinoma with extensive areas of necrosis, tumor measuring 5.5 cm. The Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social informed the patient of available therapeutic alternatives, but the patient stated he would evaluate the offered treatment and decide at his next appointment, remaining under surveillance and indicating he would initiate treatment with a private physician. His private physician, hematologist-oncologist Dr. Mario Fredy Sandoval Castañeda (colegiado 5,215), prescribed Axitinib (Inlyta) 5 mg one tablet every 12 hours indefinitely and Keytruda (Pembrolizumab) 100 mg per unit, dose 200 mg infusion over 30 minutes every 21 days. The Instituto denied supply of these medications. The patient filed the amparo on 1 December 2023, requesting supply of the medications prescribed by his private physician.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: NOT FOUND explicitly; the private treating physician characterized the disease as requiring the medications on an urgent basis ('imperative not to suspend due to effectiveness'), and the court found that denial of the medications endangered the patient's health and life
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Right nephrectomy (private sector) for renal mass of 5.5 cm with extensive areas of necrosis
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND explicitly; the patient decided to seek treatment with a private physician after the Instituto offered therapeutic alternatives
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Hematologist-oncologist Dr. Mario Fredy Sandoval Castañeda (colegiado 5,215) provided a medical report (without a medical tax stamp, which the Institute contested) and a prescription for Axitinib (Inlyta) 5 mg every 12 hours indefinitely and Keytruda (Pembrolizumab) 200 mg infusion every 21 days. The Ministerio Público stated that omitting to provide these medications could cause irreversible effects on the patient's health. The court held that the prescription alone, even without the medical report (which lacked the required tax stamp), provided sufficient professional backing to support the supply of the medications.
fields.URGENCY: Urgent — the prescribing physician stated the treatment must begin on 2 December 2023 and that it is imperative not to suspend it due to its effectiveness; the court confirmed that exhausting administrative remedies was not required
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: High risk — denial of the medications was found to endanger the patient's health; the Ministerio Público stated omission could cause irreversible effects
fields.RISK_DEATH: High risk — the court found that the refusal to supply the medications endangered the patient's life
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Axitinib (Inlyta) 5 mg one tablet every 12 hours indefinitely and Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg infusion over 30 minutes every 21 days, as prescribed by Dr. Mario Fredy Sandoval Castañeda (colegiado 5,215)
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Court confirmed the first-instance amparo and held that the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social must supply the medications prescribed by the private physician, applying the dispositivo principle. The Court found that even though the medical report lacked the required medical tax stamp (and thus was legally invalid), the prescription alone provided sufficient professional backing to order supply of the medications, consistent with prior jurisprudence that a medical prescription is enough to support constitutional protection. The Court dismissed the Institute's arguments about procurement law restrictions and the requirement for branded medications, holding that the fundamental rights to life and health prevail over administrative and economic considerations. The Court also rejected the Institute's contention that no formal denial had occurred, noting that the patient's fear of not receiving the medications constituted a credible threat sufficient to trigger amparo protection.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala, artículos 1°, 2°, 3°, 93, 94, 100, 265, 268, 272 literal c); Ley de Amparo, Exhibición Personal y de Constitucionalidad, artículos 1°, 5°, 6°, 8°, 10, 42, 43, 45, 49, 60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 66, 67, 149, 163 literal c), 185; Acuerdo 1-2013 de la Corte de Constitucionalidad, artículos 35, 36, 46; Pacto Internacional de Derechos Económicos Sociales y Culturales, artículo 12; Declaración Americana de Derechos y Deberes del Hombre, artículo XI; Ley del Timbre Médico, Decreto 3-73, artículo 4; jurisprudencia de la Corte de Constitucionalidad: expedientes 355-92, 359-92, 1471-2023, 1474-2023, 1476-2023, 1058-2023, 19-2023, 1956-2023, 1479-2023, 5016-2023, 7817-2023, 4657-2023, 6246-2023, 6798-2023, 325-2023, 2222-2023, 3901-2023, 1058-2023, 1594-2023, 1611-2023, 1471-2023, 20-2023, 2123-2023

missing_from_no: Column O mentions post-right nephrectomy for a 5.5 cm mass with areas of necrosis, which the document confirms. However, important information absent from N/O includes: (1) patient age (not stated in document either); (2) the specific medications requested (Axitinib/Pembrolizumab combination); (3) the treating physician and his specialty (hematologist-oncologist); (4) the urgency and start date of treatment (2 December 2023); (5) the Institute's contested absence of a formal denial and the patient's own decision to defer the Institute's offered treatment; (6) the legal dispute over the medical report lacking a tax stamp; (7) the judicial decision and grounds; (8) metastatic status (not confirmed in document).
possibly_incorrect: Column N identifies the diagnosis as 'Papillary Transitional Cell Carcinoma [Urothelial]' — the document consistently uses 'Carcinoma Papilar de Células Transicionales (Uroteliales)' which translates directly to this. Column O describes post-right nephrectomy for a 5.5 cm mass with areas of necrosis — the document confirms this (Instituto's report: 'fue llevado a Nefrectomía derecha en lo privado por masa renal de 5.5 mg. Resultado de patología con Carcinoma Papilar de Células Transicionales (Uroteliales), con áreas extensas de Necrosis, tumor de 5.5 cm'). Note: the document uses 'mg' which is an obvious typographical error for 'cm'. No substantive conflict detected.

rows.O: Metastatic to lung, lymph nodes, and liver. Adjuvant treatment.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Malignant melanoma Stage Three-D (IIID), specifically nodular pigmented melanoma with extensive areas of clear cells — TNM staging T1b, N3, M0
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Patient Otto René Gómez Ramírez, 54 years old, affiliate of the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social. Presented with a mole on the first toe of the right foot with 3 years of evolution, noting accelerated growth in recent weeks. Consulted a dermatologist who performed a biopsy on 14 February 2022 confirming nodular pigmented melanoma with extensive areas of clear cells, neoplasia of 0.8 cm, infiltrating adipose tissue with deep border involvement. Diagnosis: malignant melanoma Stage Three-D (T1b, N3, M0). The patient was first seen by oncologist Dr. César Estuardo Hernández Monroy on 11 May 2022. Current diagnosis per the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social: malignant melanoma Stage Three metastatic to pulmonary, lymph nodes and liver. The treating physician recommended adjuvant treatment with Pembrolizumab given the complete resection and lymph node involvement. The patient sent a letter to the Institute's health prestations submanagement requesting the medication; receiving no response, he filed amparo.
fields.SYMPTOMS: History of a mole on the first toe of the right foot with 3 years of evolution and accelerated growth in recent weeks
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Malignant melanoma Stage Three-D (T1b, N3, M0) with complete resection and lymph node involvement, requiring adjuvant immunotherapy to increase progression-free survival; the Instituto recorded the current diagnosis as Stage Three metastatic to pulmonary, lymph nodes and liver
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Biopsy on 14 February 2022; complete surgical resection (implied by physician's description of 'complete resection' with lymph node involvement)
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND explicitly in the document
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Per the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social's current diagnosis: lung, lymph nodes, and liver. Per the treating physician's medical certificate: lymph node involvement (N3) — the specific distant metastatic organs were described as lung, lymph nodes and liver by the Instituto
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Oncologist Dr. César Estuardo Hernández Monroy (colegiado 9,101) certified that the patient has malignant melanoma Stage Three-D (T1b, N3, M0) and recommended adjuvant treatment with Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg every 3 weeks by intravenous infusion of 30 minutes for 18 doses (1 year), noting that this treatment has demonstrated a significant increase in progression-free survival in patients with lymph node involvement after complete resection, as in the present case.
fields.URGENCY: Urgent — the patient requested the medication urgently citing the gravity of the disease; the court confirmed that exhausting administrative remedies was not required given the sensitivity and gravity of the rights at stake
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: High risk — malignant melanoma is described as one of the most serious and aggressive cancers; without adjuvant immunotherapy, disease progression was imminent
fields.RISK_DEATH: High risk — the patient and his physician described the risk as life-threatening; the court confirmed that the right to life was at risk if the medication was not supplied
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks, 30-minute infusion, for 18 doses (1 year of treatment), as prescribed by Dr. César Estuardo Hernández Monroy (colegiado 9,101)
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Court upheld the first-instance amparo, finding that the patient's right to life and health were at risk due to the Institute's failure to provide the prescribed medication. Applying the dispositivo principle, the Court held that when an affiliate presents a medical certificate and prescription from a specialist oncologist documenting the diagnosis and recommending specific immunotherapy with scientific justification (increased progression-free survival in patients with lymph node involvement post-resection), it is appropriate to honor the patient's preference for the medication under the responsibility of the patient and treating physician. The Court dismissed the Institute's arguments about the amparo being used to select specific medication brands, the inapplicability of the Ley de Contrataciones del Estado in the constitutional sphere, and allegations about questionable conduct by the patient's lawyer and prescribing physician, holding that these matters were irrelevant to the case at hand given the adequate medical backing. The Court further held that granting the amparo does not constitute judicial prescription of medicine but a constitutional determination grounded in the expert physician's recommendation.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala, artículos 1°, 2°, 3°, 93, 94, 95, 100, 265, 268, 272 literal c); Ley de Amparo, Exhibición Personal y de Constitucionalidad, artículos 8°, 10, 42, 43, 45, 49, 60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 66, 67, 149, 163 literal c), 185; Acuerdo 1-2013 de la Corte de Constitucionalidad, artículos 36, 46; Pacto Internacional de Derechos Económicos Sociales y Culturales, artículo 12; Declaración Americana de Derechos y Deberes del Hombre, artículo XI; jurisprudencia de la Corte de Constitucionalidad: expedientes 1042-2024, 1749-2024, 3603-2024, 4615-2023, 3741-2021, 1749-2024, 1245-2024, 3816-2024, 3603-2024, 2543-2023, 7164-2022

missing_from_no: Column O states 'Metastatic to lung, lymph nodes, and liver. Adjuvant treatment.' — this information comes from the Instituto's current diagnosis recorded in the informe circunstanciado. The document also contains important information not captured in N/O: (1) patient age (54 years); (2) TNM staging (T1b, N3, M0) and sub-classification (Stage Three-D); (3) histopathological type (nodular pigmented melanoma with extensive areas of clear cells, neoplasia 0.8 cm infiltrating adipose tissue); (4) biopsy date (14 February 2022) and onset history (mole on right foot first toe, 3 years evolution with recent accelerated growth); (5) complete surgical resection as prior treatment; (6) the specific dosing regimen (18 doses over 1 year); (7) scientific justification for immunotherapy (increased progression-free survival in patients with lymph node involvement post-resection); (8) urgency; (9) judicial decision and grounds.
possibly_incorrect: Column O describes the disease as 'Metastatic to lung, lymph nodes, and liver' — this reflects the Instituto's own current diagnosis ('Melanoma Maligno Etapa III Metastásico a Pulmonares, Ganglios e Hígado'). However, the treating physician's medical certificate classifies the case as Stage Three-D (T1b, N3, M0), which in standard TNM means regional lymph node involvement without distant metastasis (M0). There is a potential inconsistency between the Instituto's description ('metastatic to lung and liver') and the TNM M0 staging in the certificate. Column O may be inaccurate or reflect a different clinical assessment point; Column N ('MALIGNANT MELANOMA STAGE III D') accurately reflects the staging in the physician's certificate.

rows.N: Acral metastatic melanoma clinical stage IV
rows.O: Lesion on the left big toe with metastases in inguinal lymph nodes and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathies (abdomen) larger than 2 cm.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Acral metastatic melanoma, clinical Stage Four (TX N2 M1a)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Patient Carlos García Tohom, 74 years old, affiliate of the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social. History of lentiginous lesion on the first toe of the left foot accompanied by clinically palpable inguinal lymph nodes. Biopsy of the inguinal lymph nodes confirmed by immunohistochemistry as metastasis of melanoma. Imaging studies revealed multiple retroperitoneal lymphadenopathies larger than 2 centimeters. The Instituto provided treatments including Temozolomide (Temozolamida), which was started approximately one month before the amparo filing (17 January 2024); the patient had received only one dose when he filed the amparo on 15 February 2024. The treating physician, Dr. Luis Estuardo Orellana Urzúa (colegiado 19,050), issued a medical certificate on 12 October 2023 certifying the diagnosis and recommending urgent initiation of Keytruda (Pembrolizumab) 200 mg intravenously every 21 days, indefinitely, until toxicity or disease progression.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Clinically palpable inguinal lymph nodes; lentiginous lesion on the left foot first toe
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Acral metastatic melanoma, clinical Stage Four (TX N2 M1a) with inguinal lymph node metastases and multiple retroperitoneal lymphadenopathies larger than 2 cm; prior treatments including Temozolomide were ineffective or insufficiently evaluated
fields.FUNCTIONAL_LIMITATIONS: The patient stated he was 'limited' in his ability to acquire the required medications due to economic or access constraints
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Temozolomide (started by the Instituto approximately one month before the amparo, with only one dose administered); other unspecified treatments mentioned by the patient as ineffective or causing adverse effects
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: The patient stated that various prior procedures and medications prescribed by his treating physician had not produced beneficial results and had caused adverse effects; he indicated that after years of struggling with the condition he had progressed to the point of fearing for his life
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Inguinal lymph nodes (confirmed by immunohistochemistry biopsy); multiple retroperitoneal lymphadenopathies larger than 2 centimeters
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Dr. Luis Estuardo Orellana Urzúa (colegiado 19,050) certified that the patient has acral metastatic melanoma Stage Four (TX N2 M1a) with history of lentiginous lesion on the left foot first toe, inguinal lymph node metastases confirmed by immunohistochemistry, and multiple retroperitoneal lymphadenopathies larger than 2 cm. The physician recommended urgent initiation of Keytruda (Pembrolizumab) 200 mg intravenously every 21 days indefinitely until toxicity or disease progression. The Ministerio Público stated that failing to provide the medication could cause irreversible effects on the patient's health.
fields.URGENCY: Urgent — the physician explicitly recommended urgent initiation of the medication; the court confirmed that exhausting administrative remedies was not required
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: High risk — Stage Four acral metastatic melanoma with multiple lymphadenopathies; prior treatments were reported as ineffective; the Instituto's Temozolomide had not been given sufficient time to demonstrate efficacy
fields.RISK_DEATH: High risk — the patient stated he feared losing his life; the court confirmed that the right to life was at risk
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg intravenously every 21 days, indefinitely, until toxicity or disease progression, as prescribed by Dr. Luis Estuardo Orellana Urzúa (colegiado 19,050)
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Court upheld the first-instance amparo, applying the dispositivo principle and holding that the certificate and prescription issued by the private treating physician provided sufficient scientific backing to order the Instituto to supply Pembrolizumab. The Court dismissed the Institute's argument that the amparo was moot because the Institute had already begun supplying the medication, finding that the contested act remained active and no express waiver of the petition had been filed. The Court also rejected the argument that insufficient time had been allowed for Temozolomide to show pharmacological results, holding that under the dispositivo principle the patient's preference for a specific medication — backed by medical support — must be privileged. Administrative procurement restrictions and the prohibition on purchasing branded medications were dismissed as insufficient grounds to deny the constitutional protection given the primacy of the rights to life and health.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala, artículos 1°, 2°, 3°, 93, 94, 100, 265, 268, 272 literal c); Ley de Amparo, Exhibición Personal y de Constitucionalidad, artículos 8°, 10, 42, 43, 45, 49, 60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 66, 67, 149, 163 literal c), 185; Acuerdo 1-2013 de la Corte de Constitucionalidad, artículos 36, 46; Pacto Internacional de Derechos Económicos Sociales y Culturales, artículo 12; Declaración Americana de Derechos y Deberes del Hombre, artículo XI; jurisprudencia de la Corte de Constitucionalidad: expedientes 2663-2023, 3069-2023, 3766-2023, 1391-2023, 7994-2023, 4696-2023, 4415-2023, 3972-2023, 4696-2023, 3972-2023, 1472-2023, 4696-2023, 2967-2023, 5023-2023, 2663-2023, 4127-2015

missing_from_no: Column O partially captures the metastatic sites (inguinal lymph nodes and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathies). Important information absent from N/O includes: (1) patient age (74 years); (2) the specific TNM staging (TX N2 M1a); (3) the immunohistochemistry confirmation of inguinal lymph node metastasis; (4) the primary lesion location (first toe of the left foot — not the right foot as might be inferred from some texts); (5) prior treatments (Temozolomide started by the Instituto, other unspecified treatments); (6) the 12 October 2023 date of the medical certificate; (7) urgency and risk to life; (8) the Instituto's argument about Temozolomide not having been given sufficient time to demonstrate efficacy; (9) judicial decision and grounds.
possibly_incorrect: Column O states 'Lesion on the left big toe' — the document consistently refers to 'primer artejo del pie izquierdo' (first toe of the left foot), which is consistent. Column O mentions 'metastases in inguinal lymph nodes and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathies (abdomen) larger than 2 cm' — both confirmed in the document. Column N states 'Acral metastatic melanoma clinical stage IV' — the document confirms 'MELANOMA ACRAL METASTÁSICO ESTADIO CLÍNICO IV (TX N2 M1a)'. No factual conflicts detected.

rows.N: Renal Cell Carcinoma (Renal Cancer) Clinical Stage IV, with bone metastasis

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Renal cancer, clinical Stage Four (IV) — confirmed as renal cell carcinoma
fields.SECONDARY_DIAGNOSES: Pulmonary lesions with evidence of tumor activity at distance (distant pulmonary involvement)
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: Patient Alejandro García Menéndez, affiliate of the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social. In June 2023, began experiencing discomfort on the left side of the hip and consulted a traumatologist who ordered laboratory exams (computed tomography and hip radiography, among others); the radiography revealed a spot on a bone. A thoracic and chest computed tomography was performed along with blood tests, which yielded a preliminary diagnosis of a tumor in the right kidney; a biopsy was subsequently performed confirming 'renal cell carcinoma.' The patient was diagnosed with 'cancer' and began attending the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social for treatment. Due to perceived deficiencies in the Instituto's treatment, he consulted private oncology specialist Dr. Eduardo Gharzouzi (colegiado 9,939), who confirmed a final diagnosis of renal cancer, clinical Stage Four, with evidence of tumor activity at distance at the pulmonary level. Dr. Gharzouzi recommended Keytruda (Pembrolizumab) 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks and Inlyta (Axitinib, 5 mg) one tablet every 12 hours, both to be administered until disease progression or toxicity. The patient attempted to submit a written request to the Instituto for the prescribed medications but the Instituto refused to receive it; a verbal request was also made without a positive response. Amparo was filed on 21 December 2023.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Discomfort on the left side of the hip (June 2023); spot on bone visible on radiography
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Renal cell carcinoma, clinical Stage Four with evidence of tumor activity at distance at the pulmonary level; requiring combination immunotherapy and targeted therapy
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Biopsy; treatment provided by the Instituto Guatemalteco de Seguridad Social (unspecified medications accorded to the pathology, as stated by the Instituto); no specific prior systemic oncological treatment documented beyond what the Instituto was providing
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: The patient perceived deficiencies in the Instituto's treatment, prompting consultation with a private specialist; the Instituto confirmed the diagnosis only in December 2023 and had not prescribed the medication that the oncologist deemed appropriate
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 %: NOT FOUND | Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND | Tumor Mutational Burden: NOT FOUND | Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2: NOT FOUND | Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor: NOT FOUND | Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase: NOT FOUND | ROS1: NOT FOUND | KRAS: NOT FOUND | B-Raf Proto-Oncogene: NOT FOUND | others: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Pulmonary (evidence of tumor activity at distance at the pulmonary level, per Dr. Gharzouzi's medical certificate); initial bone finding on hip radiography that prompted diagnostic workup
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Dr. Eduardo Gharzouzi (colegiado 9,939), Specialist in Oncology, certified in his medical certificate of 4 December 2023 that the patient has renal cancer, clinical Stage Four, with evidence of distant tumor activity at the pulmonary level, and recommended initiating Keytruda (Pembrolizumab) 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks and Inlyta (Axitinib, 5 mg) one tablet every 12 hours, both until disease progression or toxicity. Two prescriptions for these medications were also issued. The court and the patient's advocates noted that these medications are necessary to stop the advance of the cancer and preserve quality of life.
fields.URGENCY: Urgent — the patient stated the medications are needed to stop the cancer's advance; the court confirmed that exhausting administrative remedies was not required given the gravity of the rights to life and health
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: High risk — renal cancer Stage Four with pulmonary metastatic activity; untreated disease would progress and worsen
fields.RISK_DEATH: High risk — the court found that the failure to provide the medications threatened the patient's right to life; the Ministerio Público stated that omission to provide the medications would put the patient's health and life at grave risk
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg intravenously every 3 weeks combined with Axitinib (Inlyta) 5 mg one tablet every 12 hours, both administered until disease progression or toxicity, as prescribed by Dr. Eduardo Gharzouzi (colegiado 9,939)
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The Constitutional Court upheld the first-instance amparo, applying the dispositivo principle and holding that when an affiliate has adequate medical support — here a certificate and two prescriptions from a specialist oncologist confirming Stage Four renal cancer with pulmonary tumor activity at distance — the patient's preference for specific medications must be honored under the responsibility of the patient and the treating physician. The Court incorporated, as a preliminary matter, extensive jurisprudence of the Inter-American Court of Human Rights and the Committee on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights regarding the content and scope of the rights to health and social security, concluding that these standards are binding on Guatemala through conventional control. The Court dismissed the Institute's arguments regarding the Ley de Contrataciones del Estado, lack of passive legitimation, failure to exhaust administrative remedies (definitividad), and the contention that only the Institute's physicians can prescribe medications, holding that fundamental rights to life and health prevail over all such administrative and formal objections.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Constitución Política de la República de Guatemala, artículos 1°, 2°, 3°, 93, 94, 95, 96, 100, 265, 268, 272 inciso c); Ley de Amparo, Exhibición Personal y de Constitucionalidad, artículos 1°, 5°, 6°, 8°, 49, 50, 60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 149, 163 inciso c), 179, 185; Acuerdo 3-89, artículo 7 Bis; Acuerdo 1-2013 de la Corte de Constitucionalidad, artículos 35, 36, 46; Pacto Internacional de Derechos Económicos Sociales y Culturales, artículos 9 y 12 (Observaciones Generales No. 14 y No. 19 del Comité DESC); Declaración Americana de Derechos y Deberes del Hombre, artículo XI; Convención Americana sobre Derechos Humanos, artículos 1.1, 2, 4, 5.1; Carta de la OEA, artículo 45; Declaración Universal de Derechos Humanos, artículos 22 y 25; Corte IDH: Caso Manuela y otros Vs. missing_from_no: Column O is marked 'NI' (no information). The document contains extensive information not captured in N/O: (1) patient age (not stated in document); (2) the symptom onset in June 2023 (hip discomfort and bone finding on radiography as initial presentation); (3) the diagnostic sequence (computed tomography, radiography, blood tests, biopsy); (4) the specific metastatic site (pulmonary tumor activity at distance — not 'bone' as stated in Column N); (5) the treating private oncologist and his prescription (Dr. Eduardo Gharzouzi, colegiado 9,939); (6) the combination treatment regimen (Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib); (7) the Instituto's informal and verbal refusal to receive the request; (8) urgency and risk to life; (9) extensive Inter-American Court jurisprudence cited by the court; (10) judicial decision and grounds.
possibly_incorrect: Column N describes the diagnosis as 'Renal Cell Carcinoma (Renal Cancer) Clinical Stage IV, with bone metastasis.' The document does NOT confirm bone metastasis. The medical certificate states 'evidence of tumor activity at distance at the pulmonary level' (metastasis to the lung). The initial bone finding on hip radiography was the first symptom that prompted diagnostic workup, but the confirmed metastatic site documented in the oncologist's certificate is pulmonary, not bone. The description 'with bone metastasis' in Column N is likely incorrect — the bone finding was a preliminary diagnostic lead, not a confirmed metastatic site in the oncologist's assessment.
